intended_use_full: Aurora is a Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) that establishes sleep quality. Aurora automatically analyzes, displays, and summarizes Photoplethysmogram (PPG) data collected during sleep using compatible devices. Aurora is intended for use by and by order of a healthcare professional to aid in the diagnosis of sleep disorders including sleep apnea in adults.
ground_truth: Simultaneous polysomnography (PSG) manually scored by three registered polysomnographic technologists using AASM scoring guidelines following 3% desaturation guidance, with consensus requirement of at least two-thirds agreement, reviewed by board-certified sleep physician.
other_metrics: Sleep staging sensitivity/specificity: Wake 86.7%/93.5%, Light Non-REM 80.9%/85.5%, Deep Non-REM 63.4%/95.9%, REM 83.6%/97.5%. Deming regression and Bland-Altman analysis for eAHI, Total Sleep Time, Sleep Efficiency, Sleep Latency, Wake After Sleep Onset, and Oxygen Desaturation Index reported with slopes, intercepts, and limits of agreement.
